name: Expose Rust Axum Use-Case Adapter
title: Expose an Existing Use Case Through Axum
summary: Implementation-grade guidance for adding a generic Rust Axum endpoint that delegates to an existing application use case.
description: Use this task when the desired behavior already exists behind an application-layer use-case boundary and the work is to expose it over HTTP with Axum.

method.description: HTTP method to register, for example POST.

errorCases.description: Expected use-case errors that need HTTP mapping.

testCommand.description: Concrete verification command for this endpoint.

outputs.title: Axum endpoint adapter
outputs.description: Handler, DTOs, route registration, and use-case delegation changes.

outputs.title: HTTP error mapping
outputs.description: Transport-level mapping from application errors to response status and body.

outputs.title: Endpoint verification
outputs.description: Tests, builds, or review notes proving the endpoint behavior and boundary rules.

validations.title: Confirm existing use-case boundary
validations.description: Before implementation, confirm the requested behavior already exists behind an application/use-case boundary. If it does not, do not create business behavior in this task; report the missing prerequisite instead.
criteria: An existing application/use-case entry point for the behavior is identified before adding the Axum adapter.
If no such boundary exists, implementation stops and the missing application/use-case prerequisite is reported.

validations.title: Run Rust compile check
validations.description: When a Cargo workspace or package is present, run cargo check after implementation. If not feasible, report why.

validations.title: Run Rust formatter
validations.description: When a Cargo workspace or package is present, run cargo fmt --check after implementation. If not feasible, report why.

validations.title: Run Rust tests
validations.description: When a Cargo workspace or package is present, run cargo test after implementation. If not feasible, report why.

validations.title: Review boundary preservation
validations.description: Check that the endpoint stays a use-case adapter rather than a behavior implementation.

promptBlocks.reason: Use the reusable endpoint checklist for review.

passCriteria: Handler performs HTTP extraction and response mapping only.
Application use-case input and output are not Axum-specific.
Error mapping is explicit and client-facing errors are stable.
No SQL or persistence-specific logic is introduced in the HTTP handler.

blocks.markdown: Expose `{{ task.input.useCaseName }}` through an Axum `{{ task.input.method }}` route at `{{ task.input.route }}`. Use `{{ task.input.requestDto }}` for transport request input and `{{ task.input.responseDto }}` for transport success output.

items: Inspect the existing codebase to identify the use-case entry point, input type, output type, and dependencies. If no existing application/use-case boundary exists, do not create business behavior; report the missing prerequisite instead.
Find the established Axum routing, state, DTO, and error conventions before adding new structures.
Add the smallest endpoint adapter that maps HTTP input into the existing use-case contract.
Keep business decisions, persistence calls, and transaction boundaries out of the handler unless the existing architecture explicitly places them there.
Map success output into a transport response DTO and map expected errors through a transport-level error mapper.
Register the route and add or update tests that cover success and at least one expected error path when feasible.
When a Cargo workspace or package is present, run cargo check, cargo fmt --check, and cargo test; if any check is not feasible, record the reason.

blocks.reason: Defines the boundary rule this task must preserve.

blocks.markdown: Use the compact Axum handler skeleton from `recipe.rust-axum-thin-handler` as the reusable shape. Adapt names, route path, DTOs, state access, and error variants to the local codebase instead of copying the example verbatim.

blocks.reason: Error conversion belongs at the HTTP boundary.
Avoid direct persistence logic in the handler.

criteria.text: The Axum route is registered as `{{ task.input.method }} {{ task.input.route }}`.
An existing application/use-case boundary was identified before implementation; if absent, no business behavior was created and the missing prerequisite was reported.
The handler delegates behavior to `{{ task.input.useCaseName }}`.
The handler does not contain direct SQL or persistence-specific query logic.
Expected use-case errors map to stable HTTP status and response bodies: {{ task.input.errorCases }}.

`{{ task.input.testCommand }}` is run when feasible, alongside cargo check and cargo fmt --check when a Cargo workspace or package is present; each infeasible check is explicitly recorded with the reason.
